description: Fix critical bugs in ralph.sh parallel execution (run_parallel function). The bugs cause ENOENT uv_cwd errors when workers run inside git worktrees that get cleaned up, and prevent prd.json updates from propagating back to the main branch.

userStories.title: Fix worker log path and variable ordering in run_parallel
userStories.description: In the run_parallel function in ralph.sh, the worker subshell has a bug: line 408 references $wt_log before it is defined on line 410. Also the log file path must be defined OUTSIDE the subshell (before the parenthesis) and must point to SCRIPT_DIR not the worktree path, so logs survive worktree cleanup. Fix the variable ordering and move wt_log declaration before the subshell.
acceptanceCriteria: wt_log is defined BEFORE the subshell opening parenthesis '('
bash -n ralph.sh passes with no syntax errors
userStories.notes: Fixed: wt_log defined before the subshell; logs written to SCRIPT_DIR; bash -n passes.

userStories.title: Add __pycache__ and *.pyc to .gitignore
userStories.description: Workers running python compileall create __pycache__ directories that get committed. Add __pycache__/, *.pyc, and *.pyo patterns to .gitignore. Remove any existing __pycache__ from git tracking.
acceptanceCriteria: git ls-files shows no __pycache__ or .pyc files tracked

userStories.title: Prevent cleanup_worktrees trap from killing active workers
userStories.description: The INT/TERM trap on line 375 calls cleanup_worktrees which removes worktree directories. If a worker subshell is still running inside a worktree, removing it causes ENOENT uv_cwd for all Node.js tools (copilot, codex, qwen, cline, gemini, claude). Fix: the trap should first kill all worker PIDs (stored in a global array), wait for them, THEN cleanup worktrees. Create a global WORKER_PIDS array that run_parallel populates.
acceptanceCriteria: A global WORKER_PIDS=() array exists near the top of the script
run_parallel populates WORKER_PIDS with background PIDs
The INT/TERM trap first kills all WORKER_PIDS, waits, then calls cleanup_worktrees
userStories.notes: Fixed: trap now kills worker PIDs and waits before cleanup; WORKER_PIDS array added in ralph.sh

userStories.title: Add DEPLOY.md for the dashboard project
userStories.description: Create a DEPLOY.md file with complete deployment documentation: prerequisites, setup-env.sh usage, docker compose build/up commands, how to get Chatwoot user_access_token, auth flow explanation, architecture diagram (ASCII), and troubleshooting section. The dashboard runs on port 3010, API on 8000 internal, connects to Chatwoot via minha_rede Docker network.
acceptanceCriteria: Contains: prerequisites, quick start, setup-env.sh usage
Contains: how to get Chatwoot user_access_token
userStories.notes: Chatwoot URL: https://chatwoot.agrofel.com.br, Frontend port: 3010, Backend port: 8000, Network: minha_rede, Auth: Bearer token validated via GET /api/v1/profile

userStories.title: Validate parallel execution end-to-end with dry-run
userStories.description: Run bash -n ralph.sh to validate syntax. Then run ./ralph.sh --tool auto --dry-run --parallel 2 3 and verify it completes without errors. Update prd.json marking this story passed only if both validations succeed. Commit all changes.
acceptanceCriteria: No ENOENT or uv_cwd errors in output
userStories.notes: Validated: bash -n passed and ./ralph.sh --tool auto --dry-run --parallel 2 3 completed without errors.
